title: Cenostigma myabense
taxonomy: Cenostigma myabense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the tribe Phaseoleae and is a member of the genus Cenostigma. Cenostigma myabense is native to the Himalayas and is commonly found in the mountainous regions of the Indian subcontinent. The plant is a climber with a woody stem and produces small, fragrant flowers. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular studies.
uses: Cenostigma myabense has been found to have medicinal properties due to its bioactive compounds. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Cenostigma myabense is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. Additional studies have shown that the plant's extracts may also have antimicrobial properties.

intro: Cenostigma myabense, also known as Kala bel, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, Cenostigma myabense has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cenostigma myabense is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make herbal teas and capsules to leverage its health benefits.
